summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated attacker can cause complete Parse Dashboard server hang by sending a crafted HTTP request to the AI agent endpoint with a malicious regex pattern that triggers catastrophic backtracking.
expected_security_impact: Sending the crafted HTTP request should cause the Parse Dashboard server to hang indefinitely, demonstrating complete denial of service. The server will become unresponsive to all requests until manually restarted.
actual_observed_result: The PoC successfully triggered catastrophic backtracking. After sending the malicious payload to /apps/:appId/agent, the server's event loop was blocked for over 10 seconds during regex evaluation, demonstrating successful denial of service. The server remained in a hung state unable to process additional requests.
verification_method: The PoC script measures the response time of the exploit request. A response time exceeding 10 seconds with the server unresponsive during that period confirms successful ReDoS exploitation. The script also verifies the server is unable to respond to a follow-up health check request during the hang.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP request to exploit endpoint took >10 seconds to complete, confirming catastrophic backtracking
Server event loop was blocked during regex evaluation
Health check request sent during exploit execution timed out, confirming unavailability
Server logs show regex pattern was constructed and evaluated
Process remained in running state but unresponsive to new requests

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the vulnerable version, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
